what_it_does: A self-contained design-system preview card that renders the button component family (primary/outline/dashed/ghost/dark, plus sm/lg sizes and disabled state) styled entirely from CSS custom properties, for visual inspection in the gallery.
observations: Carries a @dsCard HTML comment with group/name/subtitle/viewport metadata that the gallery presumably parses.
Links shared _card.css and defines all button styling inline in a <style> block.
Five button variants: primary (layered gold gradient + inset highlights), outline (paper-tinted hairline), dashed (soft optional), ghost (transparent), dark (ink).
Heavy use of layered box-shadows (inset highlights + warm drop shadows) to give depth; transitions on background/box-shadow/border/color/transform at 220ms.
:focus-visible uses a gold-tinted 3px ring; :active nudges transform by 0.5px.
Disabled state via [disabled] and .is-disabled with opacity 0.45 and neutralized transform/hover.
Inline SVG icons (1.6 stroke) and a small currentColor dot for the dark MENU button.
standouts: All colour/radius/easing pulled from var(--…) tokens; only the shadow rgba values are hardcoded literals.
The 'gold earns attention once per surface' lead text encodes a usage discipline, not just visuals.
conventions: Follows the token-reference convention for colour/radius/motion (var(--accent-gold), var(--r-md), var(--ease-out)).
Departs by hardcoding rgba shadow values inline rather than via shadow tokens.
Uses the @dsCard metadata-comment convention shared across these preview files.
concerns: Many bespoke rgba() shadow/highlight literals are hand-set rather than tokenised, which is the drift the project warns against.
notes: A standalone gold-on-paper button gallery card; tokens drive colour/radius but the layered shadow recipes are inline literals.
questions: Should the inset-highlight / warm-shadow rgba recipes be promoted to shadow tokens rather than living inline here?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — metadata declares a Buttons preview card and the file renders exactly that.
intention_signals: To showcase the full calibrated button vocabulary as a single inspectable surface so the variants can be judged by eye against the paper/gold brand.
novelty: The explicit 'softer fills, warm shadows, inset highlights' depth recipe and the one-gold-per-surface usage rule baked into a preview.
contribution: Contributes the concrete button-variant styling vocabulary (five variants, sizes, states) and its layered-depth shadow recipe expressed against the token system.
summary_for_embedding: A token-driven HTML preview card rendering the design system's button variants (primary/outline/dashed/ghost/dark, sizes, disabled) with layered warm shadows and gold accents.
intention_for_embedding: To present the calibrated button component family for visual review within the ConceptV design-system gallery.
